rationale: A0 shows an unsigned/unverified executable ('remote-client.exe') run from the Downloads folder at High integrity by admin-01 on server-01. Within milliseconds, EV-001 records a WinRM (port 5985) connection from server-01 to workstation-01, and EV-002 shows wsmprovhost.exe (the WinRM host process) creating a PSScriptPolicyTest file on workstation-01, confirming a lateral-movement-style remote PowerShell session was established. EV-003 then shows powershell.exe on server-01 writing a '.vnc' file to C:\Windows\Temp, suggesting remote-client.exe may be orchestrating a VNC-style remote access session. The combination of an unverified binary in Downloads, high-integrity execution, immediate WinRM lateral movement, and a VNC output artifact is atypical and warrants investigation; however, no confirmed malicious payload, credential theft, or persistence mechanism is evidenced, and the activity could represent an authorized (but poorly governed) remote-admin tool.
